title: RSS 2.0, Atom 1.0, Namespaces, and Extension Policy · Program Defense
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss Go decisions for rss 2.0, atom 1.0, namespaces, and extension policy and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit non-claims, and named browser, compiler, network, XML, PostgreSQL, race, load, accessibility, deployment, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for standards-aware syndication vocabulary map. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal…
objectives: Map RSS channel and item required, optional, repeated, GUID, enclosure, category, source, date, and extension constructs.
Map Atom feed and entry identifiers, authors, links, text constructs, content, categories, dates, xml:base, and xml:lang.
Compare XML expanded names rather than prefixes and preserve unknown extension provenance without inventing semantics.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Feed Go Rss Channel Item · changed case · Quiz Feed Go Rss Channel Item
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz Go team handles case 3543 during an isolated backup restore exercise. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove RSS and Atom format semantics, expanded names, variance, and extension restraint; failed, canceled, stale, and cross-tenant operations must preserve the prior admitted state; then inject one URL, DNS, redirect, compression, entity, depth, timeout, or publisher-budget failure and reject it safely. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Map RSS channel and item required, optional, repeated, GUID, enclosure, category, source, date, and extension constructs.

lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Allowlist interpreted extensions, bound ignored markup, and surface unsupported constructs for review.

options.text: Accept the result because visible output is the highest authority. That claim does not survive the diagnose the hidden failure evidence boundary.

For diagnose the hidden failure, import one convenient trusted RSS feed, omit reader and publisher outcomes, revision, discovery and SSRF scope, Atom and namespace cases, XML and HTTP budgets, PostgreSQL constraints and transaction, schedule, lease, goroutine cleanup, tenant, accessibility, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as feed-service proof.
Run the boundary case, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Feed Go Extension Policy
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Feed Go Extension Policy: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz Go team handles case 2238 during a publisher-fairness incident rehearsal. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove RSS and Atom format semantics, expanded names, variance, and extension restraint; failed, canceled, stale, and cross-tenant operations must preserve the prior admitted state; then change one artifact, deployment, incident, backup, restore, rollback, or capacity condition and reconcile the release. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Allowlist interpreted extensions, bound ignored markup, and surface unsupported constructs for review.

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Feed Go Extension Policy.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Feed aggregation and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Feed Go Extension Policy, keep Feed Go Extension Policy connected to executable Go feed, XML, HTTP, PostgreSQL, schedule, API, reader, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Allowlist interpreted extensions, bound ignored markup, and surface unsupported constructs for review.

Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Allowlist interpreted extensions, bound ignored markup, and surface unsupported constructs for review.
